original_verified_report: States continued counting lawful ballots after Election Day in several close federal and state races, including mail, absentee, military, overseas, and provisional ballots.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that states have different rules for counting and certification, that provisional and curable ballots are routinely processed after Election Day, and that late changes in totals are part of lawful administration rather than fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed late 'ballot dumps' in Pennsylvania, California, North Carolina, Alaska, and Wisconsin showed officials were flipping down-ballot seats for Democrats.
nps_use_note: Useful seed for observing how ordinary post-election counting becomes a narrative of coordinated partisan theft.

original_verified_report: Milwaukee reported a large batch of absentee votes early on Nov. 6 because Wisconsin does not allow election workers to process absentee ballots before Election Day.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the early-morning Milwaukee vote update was expected under Wisconsin procedures and did not prove the Senate race was stolen.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: An Instagram post framed the Milwaukee update as a 4 a.m. 'ballot dump' that stole the Senate election from the Republican candidate.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring whether agents convert procedural timing into intentional fraud narratives.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that Starlink was used to tally or manipulate 2024 presidential votes; voting and tabulation systems are generally not connected to Starlink or the public internet.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and Snopes cited election officials and voting-system procedures showing that Starlink did not alter swing-state vote totals.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Threads and X users claimed Elon Musk's Starlink technology hacked or manipulated the election to benefit Donald Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how technical uncertainty becomes elite-control narrative drift.

original_verified_report: Early vote totals after Election Day were incomplete because millions of ballots were still being counted, especially in states with large mail-vote volumes.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and Snopes explained that preliminary national vote totals should not be compared to final 2020 totals while counting was still underway.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media users claimed 20 million Democratic votes disappeared, proving either the 2024 election was stolen or the 2020 election total was fake.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether agents preserve uncertainty about incomplete data.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that Democratic votes were sent into a 'black hole'; early returns were incomplete and later updated through normal counting.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated the claim Pants on Fire, explaining that vote totals were still being tabulated and no evidence showed targeted disappearance of Democratic votes.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Threads posts alleged Democratic districts were targeted and millions of votes were sent into a black hole.
nps_use_note: Useful for left-leaning election-fraud narrative evolution.

original_verified_report: The 2024 presidential results do not prove that Joe Biden's 2020 vote total was a lie; turnout and voter choices vary across elections, and 2024 totals were initially incomplete.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found that lower early Democratic totals in 2024 did not validate stolen-election claims about 2020.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Dinesh D'Souza claimed the 2024 result proved Biden's 80 million votes in 2020 were a lie.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how one election event is reframed to delegitimize a previous election.

original_verified_report: A video showed a first-time voter lawfully dropping off a ballot in Lincoln, Nebraska, not a ballot mule committing fraud.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact verified the person in the video and found no evidence of illegal ballot trafficking.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed a 'ballot mule' was caught on camera in Lincoln.
nps_use_note: Good for observing how ambiguous short videos become criminalized voting narratives.

original_verified_report: Wisconsin election officials received no reports of a Trump voter being assaulted at a polling site, and disinformation researchers linked the viral video to Russian Storm-1516 tactics.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited the Wisconsin Elections Commission, disinformation experts, and platform suspensions to show the video was not an authentic Wisconsin polling-place incident.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed CCTV captured Harris supporters assaulting a Trump voter at a Wisconsin voting station.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether agents amplify threat and intergroup hostility around elections.

original_verified_report: A Spanish-language video claiming Haitians had legal documents allowing them to vote in Georgia was fake and did not show real voters.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the video was fabricated and part of a broader disinformation pattern around Haitian immigrants and Georgia voting.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Haitians who had arrived only six months earlier already had legal documents allowing them to vote in Georgia.
nps_use_note: Useful for modeling how immigration and election legitimacy narratives fuse.

original_verified_report: CNN did not air a graphic showing Kamala Harris with more votes than Donald Trump in Texas during the 2024 election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the image was not an authentic CNN report and rated the claim Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts circulated an image purporting to show CNN reporting Harris ahead of Trump in Texas.
nps_use_note: Good for studying media-trust erosion via fabricated result visuals.

original_verified_report: The FBI did not release a video saying three linked groups were apprehended for rigging early voting by mail-in ballot.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the FBI said the video was fake and not from the agency.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts shared a fake FBI-branded video claiming groups had been arrested for mail-ballot rigging.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether institutional-looking artifacts accelerate belief change.

original_verified_report: A video showing people claiming to be Haitians voting for Harris in multiple Georgia counties was fake.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Georgia's secretary of state called the video obviously fake, and U.S. intelligence officials said it was manufactured by Russian influence actors.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Haitians had duplicate IDs and were voting for Harris in multiple Georgia counties.
nps_use_note: A strong seed for measuring group-threat amplification.

original_verified_report: A Northampton County video showed a USPS postal worker lawfully delivering mail ballots to the county elections office.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: USPS, Pennsylvania officials, and Northampton County officials identified the person as a postal worker doing routine election-mail delivery.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X users claimed the video showed a ballot mule dropping off a large amount of ballots after hours.
nps_use_note: Good seed for tracking how ordinary election labor becomes personalized suspicion.

original_verified_report: A Kentucky ballot-marking issue was investigated as an isolated user-error incident; the voter was able to cast a correctly marked ballot.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Kentucky officials said the voter tapped a small check box in the candidate text field and that the incident did not show machines blocking Trump votes.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed voting machines in Kentucky were not allowing voters to select Donald Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing escalation from a single usability problem to system-wide fraud.

original_verified_report: Election officials did not find evidence that Fordyce, Arkansas voting machines changed votes from Republican to Democrat.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that election officials rejected the claim and rated it Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook posts claimed Fordyce voting machines changed votes from Republican to Democrat.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring spread of machine-skeptic narratives outside battleground states.

original_verified_report: Votes in Shelby County, Tennessee were not being swapped by voting machines.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found no evidence that machines swapped votes from Kamala Harris to Donald Trump.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Instagram posts claimed Shelby County machines were swapping votes from Harris to Trump.
nps_use_note: Useful for comparing directional partisan versions of the same machine-flip narrative.

original_verified_report: Tarrant County voting machines were not changing Trump votes to Harris votes.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported no evidence of vote flipping in Tarrant County and rated the claim Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Facebook posts claimed Tarrant County voting machines were flipping votes from Trump to Harris.
nps_use_note: Good for examining whether local rumors become generalized institutional distrust.

original_verified_report: Whitfield County officials said a reported ballot-marking issue involved one voter, was resolved by spoiling the ballot and issuing a replacement, and did not indicate a machine problem.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact and AP cited county officials and Georgia officials saying the case reflected voter error and no Dominion machine was taken out of service.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Marjorie Taylor Greene claimed Dominion machines in Whitfield County were flipping votes, echoing 2020 fraud narratives.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how old election conspiracies are reactivated by new incidents.

original_verified_report: North Carolina made emergency voting adjustments after Hurricane Helene, including absentee and in-person voting accommodations, under state authority.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the changes were bipartisan, authorized, and not evidence of cheating or stealing.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media posts framed North Carolina's post-Helene voting rules as cheating or stealing.
nps_use_note: Useful for examining how crisis accommodations become legitimacy attacks.

original_verified_report: A video purporting to show Trump ballots being destroyed in Bucks County was fabricated; the ballot materials were not authentic.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: Bucks County officials called the video fake, and U.S. intelligence agencies said it was manufactured and amplified by Russian actors.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Posts claimed an election worker ripped up Trump mail ballots while preserving Harris ballots.
nps_use_note: Strong seed for simulating high-emotion fraud narratives with visual evidence.

original_verified_report: All states certified their 2024 presidential election results; there was no evidence that 22 states would refuse to certify the election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed the Jan. 5, 2025 Threads claim that '22 states will not be certifying the (2024) election' as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Threads posts claimed 22 states would not certify the 2024 election.
nps_use_note: Useful for post-election certification legitimacy drift.

original_verified_report: By Jan. 7, 2025, the 2024 presidential vote was not still being counted in the way Donald Trump described.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Trump's Jan. 7, 2025 claim that 'they're still counting the (2024 election) vote in some areas' as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump said officials were still counting the 2024 vote in some areas, implying unresolved election irregularity.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether agents distinguish certification timelines from unresolved fraud.

original_verified_report: Videos did not show Donald Trump admitting he rigged the 2024 election during his pre-inauguration day rally.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed the Jan. 19, 2025 social media claim that Trump admitted to rigging the 2024 election as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts reframed rally remarks as Trump confessing that the 2024 election was rigged.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying quotation drift and confession narratives.

original_verified_report: CBS News and 60 Minutes did not replace Kamala Harris' interview answers with completely different answers from another part of the interview in the way Trump claimed.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Trump's Feb. 6, 2025 claim about CBS replacing Harris' interview answers as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump claimed CBS and 60 Minutes swapped Harris' answers for better ones, framing media editing as election manipulation.
nps_use_note: Useful for measuring media-bias and institutional-trust drift.

original_verified_report: There is no evidence that the Biden administration used immigration policy to import voters; federal voting requires citizenship, and naturalization takes years.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited removals and expulsions data, citizenship requirements, and experts who said the claim had no evidentiary basis.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Elon Musk called Biden's immigration policy a giant voter importation scam.
nps_use_note: Useful for simulating durable post-election narratives about demographic replacement.

original_verified_report: A Rockland County, New York precinct showing zero votes for Kamala Harris did not prove Elon Musk hacked the 2024 presidential election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained the local result and found no evidence of a Musk-linked hack.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social media posts claimed zero Harris votes in one precinct proved Elon Musk hacked the election.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing escalation from local outlier to nationwide manipulation claim.

original_verified_report: A video did not show President Donald Trump admitting he became president because of a rigged 2024 election.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed the March 9, 2025 social media claim as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts claimed video showed Trump admitting the 2024 election was rigged in his favor.
nps_use_note: Useful for simulating quote and clip decontextualization.

election_context: CISA and 2020 election-security dispute revived in 2025
original_verified_report: The 2020 election was not rigged, and former CISA Director Chris Krebs did not baselessly dismiss election malfeasance.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Trump's April 9, 2025 memo claim about the 2020 election and Krebs as Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump's memo revived the claim that 2020 was rigged and that Krebs wrongly dismissed malfeasance.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether old fraud narratives contaminate new election-security debates.

original_verified_report: Arizona counties agreed to seek federal help verifying citizenship status for federal-only voters; they did not agree to remove 50,000 noncitizens from voter rolls.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reported that the Fox News headline and social posts mischaracterized the settlement; federal-only voters are not necessarily noncitizens.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed Arizona Democrats needed a lawsuit to force removal of 50,000 noncitizens from voter rolls.
nps_use_note: Good seed for omission-sensitive legal-procedure distortion.

election_context: Tax and spending bill election-cancellation rumor
original_verified_report: The Republican tax and spending bill did not give President Trump legal power to delay or cancel elections.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found no provision allowing the president to cancel or delay elections; only Congress can change the presidential election date.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Social posts claimed the bill would let Trump delay or cancel elections legally.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how legislative complexity becomes regime-threat narrative.

election_context: 2016 transition and coup narrative revived in 2025
original_verified_report: There is no evidence that Barack Obama tried to lead a coup with Hillary Clinton after Trump's 2016 victory.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Trump's July 22, 2025 claim as Pants on Fire.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump claimed Obama and Clinton tried to lead a coup after the 2016 election.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how historical election narratives are repurposed in current politics.

original_verified_report: The United States is not the only country that uses mail-in voting; international democracy data show many countries or territories allow postal voting.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited an October 2024 international report finding 34 countries or territories allow mail-in voting.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump claimed the U.S. is the only country in the world that uses mail-in voting and linked it to fraud.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring global-comparison distortions.

original_verified_report: Voting machines do not inherently delay results for two weeks, and hand-counted paper ballots would not guarantee election-night results.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited election experts and evidence that tabulators are faster and more accurate than hand counts in most large jurisdictions.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump said voting machines mean results in two weeks, while paper ballots would produce results that night.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying procedural reform narratives generated from distrust.

original_verified_report: Sacramento County vote-by-mail envelope holes were accessibility and processing features; there was no evidence they were used to discard votes based on Prop 50 choices.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained the envelope design and rated the claim misleading because ballot marks could be visible in some folds but did not reveal how a person voted.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed holes in Prop 50 ballot envelopes made it easy for Democrats to discard 'no' votes.
nps_use_note: Useful for visual-design ambiguity and fraud inference.

election_context: 2025 New York City mayoral ballot fusion voting
original_verified_report: New York City mayoral candidates appeared more than once on the ballot because New York permits fusion voting, where candidates can be nominated by multiple parties.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained that listing candidates twice was part of New York ballot law, not a scam.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Elon Musk claimed the NYC ballot form was a scam because candidates appeared twice.
nps_use_note: Good for testing whether agents understand local electoral institutions.

election_context: New York voter ID and multiple-precinct claim
original_verified_report: New York's voter ID rules do not mean people can simply lie about their address and vote in multiple precincts.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed the Nov. 2, 2025 X claim that New York has no ID requirement and people could vote in multiple precincts as barely true, indicating missing context and overstatement.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed New York's lack of a routine ID requirement lets people vote in multiple precincts by lying about addresses.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing how partial legal truth becomes fraud narrative.

original_verified_report: California's Nov. 2025 redistricting vote was not proven rigged by the evidence offered by the White House or Trump.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact found the White House misrepresented voter data, pointed to one fraud charge among millions of voters, and did not prove a statewide conspiracy.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump claimed voting in California was rigged and under serious legal and criminal review.
nps_use_note: Useful for state-level election legitimacy distortion.

original_verified_report: Medicaid enrollment does not automatically give people the right to vote or register them to vote; the National Voter Registration Act requires voter-registration services at public assistance offices.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact cited the NVRA, EAC data, and state officials showing that Medicaid offices may offer registration services but do not confer voting rights.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Mehmet Oz claimed signing someone up for Medicaid also gives them the right to vote and creates political patronage.
nps_use_note: Good for welfare-policy to election-manipulation narrative drift.

original_verified_report: A video showing a vacant lot at a Venice, California address where 26 voters were registered did not expose voter fraud.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Benny Johnson's Jan. 16, 2026 X claim about the vacant lot and 26 registered voters as false.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts framed a registration-address irregularity as proof of voter fraud.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether agents infer fraud from address anomalies.

election_context: Debunked Pennsylvania mail-ballot statistic revived in 2026
original_verified_report: Pennsylvania did not send out 1.8 million mail ballots and receive back 2.5 million in the 2020 election; the claim mixed primary and general election statistics.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: FactCheck.org cited Pennsylvania's final report and MIT election expert Charles Stewart explaining the statistic mix-up.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump and Musk amplified a debunked claim that Pennsylvania received more mail ballots than it sent out.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying persistence of corrected misinformation.

election_context: DHS SAVE citizenship-check tool and voter rolls
original_verified_report: The revamped federal SAVE tool may help states investigate voter eligibility, but evidence shows noncitizen voting is rare and the tool can wrongly flag naturalized citizens if data are outdated.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact reviewed Florida and other states' use of SAVE, noting small numbers of likely noncitizens and concerns about stale data for naturalized citizens.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: A lawmaker said DHS was pushing states to ban naturalized citizens from voting by falsely labeling them illegal.
nps_use_note: Good for balanced simulation of election-integrity tools and disenfranchisement fears.

original_verified_report: Homeland Security's CISA helps protect election infrastructure on request but does not run or oversee state vote counting and tabulation.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained CISA's limited support role and cited election-law experts saying DHS has no independent authority over how states administer elections.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Kristi Noem's remarks about ensuring the 'right people' vote were interpreted as DHS taking control over election outcomes.
nps_use_note: Useful for institutional-power narrative drift.

original_verified_report: Claims that buying beer is more protected by ID rules than voting oversimplify both alcohol-purchase rules and varied state voter ID requirements.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact explained that about two-thirds of states request or require ID to vote and that retail alcohol ID rules also vary.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: SAVE Act supporters argued that the U.S. protects beer purchases more than ballots.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying catchy analogy amplification.

original_verified_report: The SAVE America Act would not require every current voter to reregister, but it could affect people who newly register or update registration after moving or changing names.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated Mark Kelly's statement Half True: he exaggerated universal reregistration but was more accurate that ordinary driver's licenses and REAL IDs often do not prove citizenship.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Mark Kelly said the SAVE Act requires everyone to reregister in person and common IDs are not good enough.
nps_use_note: Good for measuring whether agents retain policy scope and uncertainty.

election_context: SAVE America Act in-person registration debate
original_verified_report: The SAVE America Act would require documentary proof of citizenship to be presented in person, but the share of Americans who register in person is higher than 5% under broader counting methods.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated Schumer's claim Half True, finding the bill's in-person requirement real but the 5% figure understated in-person registration depending on DMV classification.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Schumer said the SAVE Act would force Americans to register only in person, something only 5% do today.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing numerical precision in partisan messaging.

election_context: SAVE Act documentary proof of citizenship
original_verified_report: Only some driver's licenses would satisfy the SAVE America Act's documentary proof of citizenship requirement; most REAL IDs do not themselves prove citizenship.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: AP Fact Focus explained that enhanced driver's licenses from a few states may qualify, while most standard driver's licenses and REAL IDs do not show citizenship.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Public debate alternately framed the SAVE Act as either simple voter ID or as making ordinary IDs completely useless for all voters.
nps_use_note: Good for observing how complex eligibility rules simplify into competing slogans.

original_verified_report: Mail voting is a legal voting method used by many voters, including Donald Trump in Florida, and does not inherently mean cheating.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact checked Trump's claim that mail-in voting means cheating and rated it Pants on Fire, noting his own mail voting and the lack of evidence for inherent fraud.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: Trump said mail-in voting means mail-in cheating.
nps_use_note: Useful for future-facing simulations about voting-method legitimacy.

original_verified_report: The claim that 83% of Americans favor photo ID and most of the civilized world requires it needs context; voter ID systems and international rules vary widely.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact listed Restoration of America's April 13, 2026 ad claim as Half True under voter ID fact checks.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: An ad claimed 83% favor requiring photo ID to vote and that most of the civilized world requires it but the U.S. does not.
nps_use_note: Useful for studying how popularity and global-normality claims legitimize policy.

election_context: Biden post-election Oval Office presence rumor
original_verified_report: Photos, videos, and schedules showed Joe Biden did enter the Oval Office after the 2024 election; FOIA door-log claims did not prove otherwise.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated the X claim Pants on Fire after reviewing photo and video evidence disproving it.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: X posts claimed newly released door logs showed Biden never entered the Oval Office after the 2024 election.
nps_use_note: Useful for post-election institutional legitimacy narratives.

election_context: Florida redistricting and partisan gerrymandering law
original_verified_report: No federal law categorically bars states from partisan gerrymandering; the U.S. Supreme Court has said federal courts cannot resolve partisan gerrymandering claims as political questions.
supporting_fact_check_evidence: PolitiFact rated Fentrice Driskell's statement false and explained that Congress or state institutions, not federal courts under current doctrine, police partisan gerrymandering.
real_world_distorted_or_reframed_variant: A Florida Democratic leader said federal law prohibits partisan gerrymandering, overstating federal legal protections.
nps_use_note: Useful for testing whether agents preserve legal nuance in redistricting debates.
